channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Trump cancels signing of bipartisan housing bill ahead of tense meeting with GOP senators
item.description: President Donald Trump abruptly canceled the signing of a bill aimed at increasing housing affordability an hour before he was due at the Capitol for the ceremony.

item.title: Cerebras CEO says margin forecast was 'misunderstood' as stock plummets after earnings
item.description: AI chipmaker Cerebras has a staggered lock-up expiration that includes some shares becoming available for trading this week.

item.title: Energy secretary says U.S. has ended Iran's ability to close Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Some 72 ships loaded with 19 million barrels of oil have passed through Hormuz in the past 24 hours, Energy Secretary Chris Wright said.

item.title: S&P 500 turns red as chip sell-off returns ahead of Micron earnings: Live updates
item.description: The S&P 500 pulled back on Wednesday as Micron Technology shares fell, with investors looking ahead to the release of chipmaker's earnings after the bell.

item.title: Toyota gains on General Motors in new U.S. sales forecast: 'GM may be looking over their shoulder'
item.description: Toyota has notably leaned into hybrid vehicles while GM and others bet on all-electric vehicles, which saw lower-than-expected adoption from consumers.

item.title: Bitcoin falls back under $60,000, hitting its lowest level since October 2024
item.description: Bitcoin, now in the eighth month of its bear market, dropped to an October 2024 low amid a pullback in tech stocks.

item.title: Buy Verizon over Alphabet if a history of Dow changes is any guide
item.description: Verizon's removal from the Dow may not be bad news for investors. Historical data shows deleted stocks often outperform the companies replacing them

item.title: Qualcomm announces AI data center CPU, signs Meta as first major customer
item.description: Qualcomm's primary business has been smartphones, which accounted for two-thirds of the company's product revenue in it's most recent quarter.

item.title: These are Schwab's top three income ideas for the rest of 2026
item.description: Collin Martin of Schwab Center for Financial Research lays out where he sees the best opportunity for income investors right now.

item.title: Micron earnings are set to send the market on a wild ride — and a new ETF may add to the volatility
item.description: The Roundhill T-REX 2X Long DRAM Daily Target ETF (RAM) is a 2x levered version of the popular DRAM ETF.

item.title: France suffers major power outage as Europe sizzles in record-breaking heat
item.description: A heat-related power outage left around 68,000 homes without electricity in western France as record-breaking heat sweeps Europe.

item.title: Federal student loans have a new interest rate discount — here's who qualifies
item.description: To qualify for the U.S. Department of Education's new interest rate discount on student loans, borrowers must meet several requirements. Here's what to know.

item.title: House passes affordable housing bill, sends it to Trump's desk
item.description: The House on Tuesday gave final passage to a bill aimed at lowering costs for homebuyers and reining in private equity.

item.title: Traders are getting worried the chip stock bull market is cracking. How to protect yourself
item.description: Options traders were buying a lot of protection on the iShares Semiconductor ETF (SOXX) on Tuesday.

item.title: Nvidia's Huang calls black market data centers made of smuggled parts a 'dead end'
item.description: Washington regulators and the Trump administration are increasingly wary of China getting ahold of advanced AI software and hardware.

item.title: Stocks making the biggest moves midday: Cerebras Systems, KB Home, Expedia, Exxon Mobil & more
item.description: Here are the companies making headlines in midday trading.

item.title: Why energy could be a great place to invest even with oil prices retreating — 5 stocks to buy
item.description: It seems that buzz is building on investing in energy stocks in the wake of the Iran conflict.

item.title: Bessent sees GDP growth booming again this year. Kalshi traders see little chance of that
item.description: Kalshi traders are not agreeing with Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent's prediction that the U.S. economy will have 3% growth this year.

item.title: New meme stock Wendy's soars 27% with trading halted at one point
item.description: While management changes can influence investor sentiment, the magnitude of the move suggests other forces may be at play.

item.title: U.S. crude oil briefly dips below $70 as tankers transit Strait of Hormuz
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump intensified pressure on oil companies to lower gasoline prices following the recent decline in crude.

item.title: This industrial play has been a quiet winner in 2026. Why the stock has more room to go
item.description: Every weekday, the Investing Club releases the Homestretch; an actionable afternoon update just in time for the last hour of trading.

item.title: How much does debt settlement cost?
item.description: A debt settlement company could substantially lower your credit card bills. Find out what (and how) they charge for their services.

item.title: Google's YouTube settles case over social media harm to children
item.description: A Los Angeles jury found Meta and Google's Youtube negligent in another social media addiction trial that ended in March.

item.title: Treasury Secretary Bessent says U.S. GDP growth can return to 3% before end of the year
item.description: Bessent sees his "3-3-3" plan as still in reach — 3% growth, a 3% deficit-to-GDP rate and a 3 million barrels per day increase in oil production.

item.title: Amazon's Zoox unveils redesigned robotaxi ahead of upcoming expansion
item.description: Zoox is updating its robotaxis as the Amazon division plots expansion in additional markets and prepares to charge for rides.

item.title: Tom Lee says this is a classic opportunity to buy the dip in chip stocks once again
item.description: He describes the downturn this week as "buyable pullback."

item.title: Slate Auto says $24,950 electric truck will be profitable; targets positive cash flow next year
item.description: EV startup Slate Auto CEO Peter Faricy told CNBC that every vehicle the company produces will be gross margin positive.

item.title: Trump claims Iran has assured U.S. there won't be tolls on the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: There will be no tolls, insurance costs, or charges of any kind for ships looking to pass through the Strait of Hormuz, Trump said.

item.title: Kalshi CEO says prediction market thinking about IPO, but not for this year
item.description: Tarek Mansour said to CNBC's "Squawk Box" it made sense for the company at the stage to think about a debut on public markets.

item.title: OpenAI unveils first chip as part of Broadcom deal in effort to 'build the full stack'
item.description: Eight months after announcing a custom chip deal, OpenAI and Broadcom are revealing their first joint project: Jalapeño.
